name: CONTRACT-BACKLOG-39
title: Add on-demand emit_configurable_alert API; refactor skills to use it instead of hardcoded rabbit-print prose
description:
ARCHITECTURAL GAP: alert text declared per-configurable in feature.json (e.g., rabbit-cage configuration[human-approval].alert-message = 'HUMAN APPROVAL BYPASS ACTIVE - Step 4 skipped' with icon 🔑, color red) is fired automatically only at Stop hook events via iterate_configurables_alerts. There is no on-demand emit API for other code paths (dispatcher mid-skill, helper scripts) to use the same centrally-declared text. Without this API, callers either duplicate the text inline (drift risk) or skip emitting any alert.

CONCRETE DUPLICATION OBSERVED (HEAD 1d10c86b):
- rabbit-cage/feature.json configuration[human-approval].alert-message: 'HUMAN APPROVAL BYPASS ACTIVE - Step 4 skipped' (icon: 🔑, color: red). Fires at Stop hook when bypass marker is present.
- rabbit-feature/skills/rabbit-feature-touch/SKILL.md:140: hardcoded '[🐇 rabbit 🐇] Step 4 SKIPPED: .rabbit-human-approval-bypass marker active. Run /rabbit-config human-approval true to turn the bypass off and require approval again.' Dispatcher emits this manually mid-touch at Step 4 when it detects the bypass marker.

Two problems: (a) text drift — different wording, no single source of truth; (b) the '[🐇 rabbit 🐇]' envelope prefix is hardcoded in skill prose instead of being rendered by rabbit_print's banner/print format, which violates the rabbit-print direct-call API contract (Inv 48).

PROPOSED FIX:

Part A — API addition (contract feature):
- Add contract.lib.runtime.emit_configurable_alert(feature_name: str, configurable_id: str, *, repo_root: str) -> dict
- Looks up the configurable in <repo_root>/.claude/features/<feature>/feature.json
- Returns a print_result (or banner_result, per the alert-message's intended format) with the alert-message.text/icon/color
- Caller passes the returned dict to rabbit_print or just prints it to stderr
- Coverage: test/test-runtime-emit-configurable-alert.py with positive (resolves to correct text) + negative (unknown feature/configurable) cases

Part B — refactor callers:
- rabbit-feature-touch SKILL.md Step 4: replace hardcoded prose with instruction to invoke emit_configurable_alert('rabbit-cage', 'human-approval') and pipe to rabbit_print
- Optionally: extend the alert-message schema in rabbit-cage's configurable to include a longer-form 'instruction' field that the dispatcher can surface (currently only text/icon/color); decide whether the actionable 'Run /rabbit-config human-approval true' guidance should live with the alert-message or stay in skill prose

Part C — audit:
- Grep all skills (.claude/features/*/skills/**/SKILL.md) and scripts for hardcoded '[🐇 rabbit 🐇]' prefixes or alert text that duplicates a feature.json configurable's alert-message
- Refactor every match to use the new API
- File follow-up B/B if any cases require schema extensions

OWNER: contract (the meta-contract architecture container; the API addition lives in contract.lib.runtime).

WHY NOT BACKLOG-38: that one scrubs Plan/BUG tags from spec bodies — purely textual. This one adds a runtime API and refactors callers — structural. Different scope, different discipline, different wave.

history.note: Part A (contract API) complete; reopening to track Part B (rabbit-feature SKILL.md refactor) and Part C (audit) per backlog item scope
TDD cycle complete (Part B: SKILL.md refactor)
